equivalence_argument: No exact mapping: current selection is parent-hash seeded but also agent-specific, so it fails the parent-hash-only formula in this invariant.

checked_queries: revival executor selection uses parent hash
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: The start index is not computed solely as u16::from_be_bytes([hash[30], hash[31]]) % len; it is XORed with the revived agent address tail bytes.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-scheduled-block-building/src/lib.rs computes seed = u16::from_be_bytes([hash_bytes[30] ^ agent_bytes[18], hash_bytes[31] ^ agent_bytes[19]]), then rotates and skips the excluded executor.
The current selector is deterministic and parent-hash influenced, but it is not the parent-hash-tail-only formula described by the invariant and falsification test.

notes: This appears superseded by invariant 549, which explicitly requires agent-specific deterministic selection.
rationale: Selection changed from block-number modulo to parent-hash tail bytes to reduce deterministic stickiness while staying reproducible across nodes. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Heartbeat revival executor selection must seed its deterministic start index from the last two bytes of the parent hash and skip the previously failed executor when possible.
suggested_falsification_tests: Given a fixed executor list, excluded executor, and parent hash, assert all nodes compute the same selected executor; change only the parent hash tail bytes and assert the starting index changes according to `u16::from_be_bytes([hash[30], hash[31]]) % len`.
summary: Revival executor selection is not parent-hash-tail-only.
test_or_falsification_candidate: With a nonzero agent tail, assert the selected index equals u16::from_be_bytes([hash[30], hash[31]]) % len; current code uses XOR with agent bytes.
title: Revival executor selection uses parent hash
